working_on: MemPalace HNSW rebuild (Issue #112) via mempalace_rebuild_hnsw.py scratch-then-swap discipline + ≤30 min flush-primitive root-cause investigation.
outcome_summary: HNSW rebuild executed via upstream `mempalace repair --mode from-sqlite --archive-existing --yes` (project tool refused exit-4 on `onnx_mini_lm_l6_v2` — sub-finding for #113). 16,775 records rebuilt zero-loss on scratch; verified cosine 0.841 on known-content query; atomic swap to live. Post-swap MCP `mempalace_search` returns vector results (not BM25 fallback). 3 backups retained. Investigation found root cause: chromadb default `hnsw:sync_threshold=1000` rarely reached by mempalace 1–3-drawers/session writes; process exit drops in-memory `index_metadata.pickle`; `client.close()` not a flush primitive. Prevention via `hnsw:sync_threshold` collection metadata is ADR-scale, filed as Issue #113. Issue #112 closed. mempalace_unavailable_acknowledged: MCP diary_read/diary_write endpoint returning -32000 internal error post-swap (likely a stale-pickle side effect from the rebuilt palace lacking `index_metadata.pickle`; will resolve once Issue #113 prevention or MCP server restart writes the pickle).

scope_delivery.note: Both deliverables from approved plan landed: (1) HNSW rebuild via upstream from-sqlite mode, scratch-verified, atomically swapped to live, MCP vector search restored. (2) Flush-primitive investigation hit 30-min cap with concrete finding (chromadb sync_threshold root cause + prevention lever via collection metadata); Outcome B per plan — flush primitive exists but ADR-scale to wire in, filed as Issue #113. Issue #112 closed at session shutdown.
